execution_protocol: Select earliest incomplete milestone with dependencies complete
Implement only that milestone scope unless blocked
Commit with M# prefix and push to origin/main
Update milestone status in this file

milestones.title: Foundation and Deployment
deliverables: React + TypeScript scaffold
Tailwind + Framer Motion integration
Core folder structure
GitHub Pages workflow
README dev/deploy instructions
validation: npm run dev
npm run build
Pages deploy succeeds from main

milestones.title: Core Tick Engine and Save Model
deliverables: Deterministic tick loop
State model for core resources/entities
localStorage persistence
Versioned save schema
validation: Reload restores run state exactly
30+ minute run stability

milestones.title: Resource and Formula Layer
deliverables: Uncapped Belief generation formula stack
Belief flow without idle-decay penalties
Influence cap/regen/cost formulas
Prophet threshold and cult cost scaling
Domain XP and investment formulas
validation: Runtime formula outputs match manifesto constants
No global softcap logic present

milestones.title: Whispers Loop (Era I)
deliverables: Whisper and recruit actions
Prophet conversion
Active event cadence hooks
Era I gate checks
validation: Active play reaches Era II gate conditions
Decision cadence remains 30-60 seconds

milestones.title: Doctrine Loop (Era II)
deliverables: Cult formation and growth
Act system and returns
Rival systems
Era II gate checks
validation: Cults and rivals create strategic tension
Era II to III gate is multi-system

milestones.title: Veil, Miracles, and Civilization Pressure (Era III)
deliverables: Veil bonus/regen/erosion/collapse
Miracle tiers and returns
Civilization health/collapse/rebuild
Unraveling gate checks
validation: Veil 30-55 yields high-risk/high-reward play
Miracle spam self-limits via civ stability
Unraveling reachable through intended path

milestones.title: Offline Progression Engine
deliverables: 8h capped offline simulation
85% offline Belief efficiency
Offline rules for Influence/Veil/Rivals
Return summary (lore + deltas)
validation: No offline collapse
Offline Veil floor enforced at 15
Return state is coherent and playable

milestones.title: Ascension and Echo Trees
deliverables: Ascension reset pipeline
Echo gain formula (divisor 750000)
Fibonacci-cost Echo trees
Early bottleneck-fix upgrades
validation: Run 2 is structurally distinct
Echo builds create viable strategic variance

milestones.title: Social Memory and Lineages
deliverables: Trait inheritance
Trust debt and skepticism modifiers
Historical markers in event outcomes
validation: Prior-run decisions alter future conversion dynamics

milestones.title: Pantheon Layer (Run 2+)
deliverables: Pantheon entities
Alliance/betrayal mechanics
Domain poisoning persistence
Name letter betrayal hook
validation: Alliance and betrayal are both viable with distinct tradeoffs

milestones.title: UI as Veil and Omen Log
deliverables: Progressive UI legibility system
Veil-driven disclosure rules
In-universe omen log baseline
validation: Readability progression is mechanically visible
Event feed avoids sterile system text

milestones.title: Audio as System Feedback
milestones.notes: Historical milestone retained for record. Audio surfaces were later removed from active runtime UX.
deliverables: Tone.js domain layers
Instability detune/dropout
Mute and fallback controls
Layered signal chain (dry bus + reverb bus + master filter)
Low-Veil drone and staged instability transitions
validation: Players hear destabilization before major visual consequences
Audio remains readable under Era III domain density

milestones.title: Ghost Echoes and Async Signatures
deliverables: Run signature format
Import/export workflow
Anomaly injection and fallback behavior
validation: Imported signatures produce recognizable world fingerprints
Core game complete without signature data

milestones.title: Architecture Layer and Remembrance Endgame
deliverables: Meta-rule editing layer
All name letter conditions and tracking
Secret final choice flow
validation: Remembrance path completable
Final choice remains intentionally ambiguous

milestones.title: Balance and Runtime Targets
deliverables: Timing and economy tuning for Revision B targets
Decision and milestone cadence instrumentation (per-run action cadence summary with avg/median/p90 interval and target-window hit rate)
Era pacing lock across beginner and ascended runs: Era I->II and Era II->III speed-up should be noticeable but not abrupt
Regression tests for formulas and offline sim
validation: Run timing approximates 6.5h, 4h, 2.5h, 1.2h, 45m floor
Ascended-vs-beginner session compare shows controlled acceleration (not flat, not abrupt)
No high-severity simulation regressions
milestones.notes: Feature-first ordering: execute M14 after M21 + M17 are complete. Test loop: (1) npm run regression:m14, (2) npm run compare:m14 -- <beginner-session.json> <ascended-session.json>, (3) npm run audit:m14 -- <telemetry-export-1.json> [telemetry-export-2.json ...]. Pace lock targets: compare output should show ascended speed ratios lower than 1.00 but not below 0.45 for Era I and Era II. Recommended loop: gather 2-3 beginner and 2-3 ascended exports, apply one small constant delta, then re-run compare + audit.

milestones.title: Era UI Restructure and Disclosure Consolidation
milestones.notes: Completed. Consolidated PF-02 and PF-03 scope into the era shell structure, anti-clutter flow, and strict reveal behavior.
deliverables: Era-locked shell flow: Era I minimal, Era II ACTIVE/GROWTH(+constrained META content), Era III full ACTIVE/GROWTH/META
Era II tab contract: ACTIVE order is Whispers -> Doctrine (merged); Influence is shown in the top stat bar only; GROWTH order is Domains -> Rivals -> Threshold
Era II ACTIVE containers are collapsible with persisted localStorage state
Doctrine and Doctrine Seeds merge into one collapsible Doctrine container in ACTIVE with Prophets/Cults + Lineage above Acts
All GROWTH containers are collapsible with persisted localStorage state
Era II rivals collapse to one-line status when inactive and expand to full controls when active
Era II threshold sits at the bottom of GROWTH (not ACTIVE) and defaults to collapsed
Era III GROWTH order is Doctrine (merged) -> Domains -> Rivals; Threshold remains in persistent Unraveling strip
Era II+ META containers are collapsible with persisted localStorage state
ACTIVE tab prioritizes Cataclysm, rivals, whispers, and influence pressure in Era III
Unraveling gate as persistent strip in Era III
Echo trees constrained to META view; between-run systems removed from active flow
Persistent omen surface across tabs (drawer/strip)
Theme token progression by era (data-era) for palette/texture/typography density shifts
Era composition extracted to src/ui/eras/* and shared shell surfaces to src/ui/layout/*
Shared reveal helper enforcement for future-era disclosure
validation: Era III critical actions are accessible without long scrolling
Future-era systems are hidden until unlock
Core loop screens remain coherent at 375px width without horizontal overflow

milestones.title: Save Integrity and Recovery
milestones.notes: Must precede M14 balance tuning. Players must not lose runs during stress testing or normal play due to save corruption.
deliverables: Versioned save migration pipeline for schema changes
Secondary snapshot slot: auto-saved 'last good state' before each ascension and each era transition
Manual save export to JSON file (download)
Save import with version validation and conflict warnings
Graceful corruption recovery: detect bad state, offer rollback or fresh run with explanation
validation: Corrupted localStorage does not crash the game
Exported save can be re-imported and continues correctly
Schema migration from prior version produces valid state
Ascension snapshot correctly reflects pre-reset state

milestones.title: Instrumentation and Playtesting Telemetry
milestones.notes: Must precede or run parallel with M14. Balance tuning should be evidence-based. All telemetry is local-only unless opt-in is explicitly added later.
deliverables: Structured local event log: era transitions, collapses, ascensions, rival suppression, miracle use — each with timestamp and key state snapshot
Per-run summary object written to localStorage on ascension: total time, belief earned, echo gained, peak B/s, collapse count, miracle count by tier
In-game Stats page reading and displaying run history summaries
Optional console/JSON export for manual analysis during playtesting
Hooks for M14 regression tests to read instrumentation output
validation: Era transition events are logged with correct timestamps
Per-run summary is written correctly on ascension
Stats page renders run history without performance impact
Log does not grow unbounded: cap at last 20 runs

milestones.title: Accessibility and Mobile Resilience
milestones.notes: Completed accessibility/mobile hardening pass. Added adaptive tick throttling for hidden/low-power states, coarse-pointer 44x44 touch target enforcement, reduced-motion Framer Motion disable path, keyboard-safe tab/collapse semantics, and era-transition focus handoff.
deliverables: Responsive layout audit: all panels usable at 375px width without horizontal scroll
Touch target sizing: all interactive elements meet 44x44px minimum
Tick rate throttling when tab is backgrounded or device signals low power
Reduced-motion support: disable Framer Motion animations when prefers-reduced-motion is set
Keyboard navigation for all primary actions (Whisper, Recruit, Invest, Act, Miracle)
Focus management when new panels unlock progressively
validation: Full Era I loop completable on 375px mobile viewport
No layout overflow or hidden buttons at small sizes
Background tab does not spin CPU at full tick rate
All primary actions reachable via keyboard alone
Reduced-motion mode produces no animation jank

milestones.title: New Game+ Convergence Mode
milestones.notes: Run 8+ needs structural novelty beyond compression speed. Convergence is not a difficulty mode — it is a layered game state where prior-run echoes interact in ways that alter the run's shape. Design must not break the 45-minute floor or the ambiguous final choice.
deliverables: Convergence flag unlocked after completing Remembrance path once
Ghost interference layer: imported or locally-generated prior-run signatures passively alter domain synergy, belief pressure timing, and rival spawn patterns in the active run
Accumulated betrayal/alliance history produces Pantheon disposition modifiers that compound across Convergence runs
Convergence-only omen log entries that reference prior-run events by name
Convergence does not alter core formulas or gate conditions — pressure is narrative and systemic, not numerical inflation
Optional: Convergence run summary screen showing which ghost echoes were active and their observed effects
validation: Convergence run is structurally distinct from standard run 8+
Ghost interference is perceptible but does not guarantee win or loss
Final choice presentation remains ambiguous and unresolved
45-minute floor is preserved under Convergence conditions
Convergence does not require M12 Ghost Echoes import to function (falls back to local prior-run signatures)

milestones.title: Documentation and Wiki Foundation
milestones.notes: Convert internal implementation documentation into a stable wiki-ready knowledge base and keep it synchronized with mechanics.
deliverables: Maintain docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md as formula-level implementation reference
Define wiki page map and ownership for ongoing updates
Publish first-pass wiki pages for era loop, formulas, prestige/pantheon/remembrance, and save/offline/telemetry troubleshooting
Add documentation sync validation to workflow guidance
validation: All implemented systems are documented with current formulas/constants
Internal reference and wiki pages are cross-linked and consistent
Mechanic changes are accompanied by MANIFESTO/roadmap/reference updates

milestones.title: Devotion Path System
milestones.notes: Completed path differentiation and lineage memory pass. Era II behavior now resolves Fervour/Accord emergence, Era III supports all four paths (Fervour/Accord/Reverence/Ardour), path shifts are omen-announced, run-to-run dominant-path memory seeds next-run momentum, and stats surface shows current path + stacks.
deliverables: Era II Devotion path differentiation: Fervour (act-heavy) and Accord (cult-heavy) emerge from player behavior, not explicit choice
Era III named path crystallization: Fervour, Accord, Reverence, Ardour with distinct mechanical effects
Path switching requires sustained behavior change over time (not instant)
Omen log integration names path shifts in-world
Run 2+ lineage Devotion memory grants one free starting stack toward prior dominant path threshold
Stats page shows Devotion path label and stack count
MANIFESTO.md and docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md include full path-system formulas when implemented
validation: Era II path emergence tracks act-vs-cult behavior ratios correctly
Era III path effects are distinct and meaningfully affect play without dominating it
Path switching is possible but requires sustained behavior change
Lineage memory applies correctly in run 2+
Devotion never decays and does not punish inactivity
All four Era III paths are viable builds

milestones.title: Rival Identity and Faith Competition System
milestones.notes: Rivals in current runtime are a passive drain with a single suppress action. They have no identity, no strategic depth, and no reason to engage with beyond paying 200 Influence. This milestone replaces the current rival system with a competing-faith model that has narrative identity and meaningful player decisions. Rivals should feel like actual threats with character, not resource timers. Full design reference: docs/RIVAL_IDENTITY.md (to be written).
deliverables: Each rival spawns with a procedurally generated name, a dominant domain, and one in-world sentence describing their doctrine - drawn from the same domain and lineage systems the player uses
Rival strength scales with their dominant domain level relative to the player's - rivals adapt to the player's current power rather than scaling purely from B/s

Three response options replace the single suppress button: Suppress (Influence cost, eliminates rival), Ignore (rival grows over time, higher drain but eventual collapse), Engage (Belief cost, chance to convert rival's followers to player cult)
Engage outcome is probabilistic based on player Devotion state and lineage - Fervour path gets better Engage odds, Accord path gets better Suppress cost
Rival identity persists in omen log: named rivals reference their prior appearance if suppressed and re-spawning
Rivals visible in Era II and III following existing era gate; Era II rivals feel like local competition, Era III rivals feel like organized opposition
Rival panel UI updated: shows rival name, dominant domain, current strength as relative threat level (not raw number), estimated follower drain rate, and three response buttons
First rival appearance in a run gets a one-line in-world introduction omen: the rival is introduced by what they teach, not by system label
MANIFESTO.md and GAME_REFERENCE.md updated with rival identity model and response option formulas
validation: Each rival has a distinct name and doctrine sentence on spawn
All three response options are viable with distinct tradeoffs
Engage outcome correctly applies Devotion path modifier
Rival strength display is relative threat level, not raw number
Named rival re-spawn is referenced in omen log if prior suppression exists this run
Rival system remains absent in Era I
Suppress cost and Engage cost are clearly shown before committing
Rivals never cause game-over or unrecoverable state - they are pressure, not punishment

post_final_improvements.title: Bulk Domain Investing Controls
scope: Add invest options per domain: +1, +10%, +25%, +50%, Max (Belief-bounded)
Show projected spend and resulting level/XP before confirm
Preserve single-invest option for fine control

post_final_improvements.title: Strict Era-Locked UI Disclosure
post_final_improvements.notes: This scope is formalized structurally under M20 for milestone-level validation.
scope: Hide all future-era systems until era unlock
Fix current leak: Cataclysm content must not appear in Era II
Add a shared reveal policy helper used by all panels

post_final_improvements.title: Anti-Clutter Layout Pass
post_final_improvements.notes: Promoted into M20 as a dedicated milestone. Full spec in docs/UI_RESTRUCTURE.md and docs/ERA2_TAB_REORGANISATION.md.
scope: Reduce page scroll via docked tabs/section switcher
Collapse secondary panels by default after unlock
Move non-critical diagnostics to a compact Stats page

post_final_improvements.title: Runtime Formatting in Stats Page
scope: Format run time as days/hours/minutes/seconds
Keep primary HUD free of verbose timers
Ensure formatting consistency in offline summary and stats

post_final_improvements.title: Smoother Tick and Render Feel
scope: Separate simulation tick from UI paint rate where needed
Use interpolation/smoothing for displayed counters
Profile and remove frame hitching on long sessions

post_final_improvements.title: Omen Frequency and Authenticity Pass
scope: Reduce omen volume; prioritize milestone-significant events
Generate omen prose from event context with varied sentence structures
Add cooldown + de-duplication so repeated lines are rare

post_final_improvements.title: Progressive HUD Reveal and Explainability
scope: Hide Veil/Domain aggregate metrics until relevant unlocks
Introduce each metric with brief in-world explanation
Keep early UI near-minimal per manifesto progression intent

post_final_improvements.title: Run 1 Onboarding and Tutorial Veil
scope: Design the first 20 minutes of run 1 as a deliberate pacing artifact, not an afterthought
Introduce Belief, Influence, and Whispers sequentially with contextual in-world cues rather than tutorial popups
First prophet conversion moment should be marked as a narrative beat, not a silent threshold
Suppress non-relevant UI panels for the first 5 minutes to reduce initial cognitive load
Ensure Era I gate is reachable without external documentation or prior knowledge

post_final_improvements.title: Number Legibility and Large Value Formatting
scope: Implement consistent suffix notation: K, M, B, T for displayed values throughout all panels
Belief/s display should show at least 2 significant figures at all scales
Avoid raw integers above 9999 anywhere in primary HUD
Echo costs and totals use same suffix system for consistency
Milestone: no player should need mental arithmetic to understand their current resource state

post_final_improvements.title: Retired: Faith Decay Visual Indicator
post_final_improvements.notes: Faith decay system removed from runtime. Do not implement this item.
scope: Faith decay mechanic was removed from runtime
Any future indicator work should target active mechanics only

post_final_improvements.title: Influence Spend Nudge System
scope: When Influence is within 10% of cap and no action has been taken in 45+ seconds, show a subtle ambient signal (not a notification) that spend is being lost
Signal must be in-universe: e.g., an omen log line, a visual shimmer on the Influence meter — not a UI toast or alert
Nudge is suppressed if player has no affordable actions available
Nudge frequency is capped: at most once per 90 seconds
Aligns with manifesto spend-before-cap behavior design intent

post_final_improvements.title: Rival Threat Legibility
scope: Show active rival strength as a relative threat level, not a raw number, until Era II is established
Display estimated follower drain rate alongside rival entry in the event log
Add a brief in-world description of each rival's character on first appearance (one line, not a dossier)
Suppression cost UI should show whether player can currently afford suppress vs. convert
Rival panel visibility follows era-locked disclosure rules

post_final_improvements.title: Collapse Recovery UX
scope: When Veil collapse or civ collapse occurs, present a brief narrative moment before resuming the tick — a 2-3 second pause with an in-world omen line describing what broke
Post-collapse state should clearly show what was lost (follower count before/after, prophets lost) in the omen log
Immunity timer (if Echo upgrade owned) should be visually apparent, not hidden in stats
Recovery path (what the player should do next) should be inferrable from visible UI state without documentation
Collapse must feel consequential without feeling arbitrary or unreadable

post_final_improvements.title: Echo Tree Visual Clarity
scope: Echo tree nodes should show current rank, max rank, and cost-to-next in a single compact view
Purchased nodes visually distinct from affordable/locked/unavailable states
Fibonacci cost progression should be readable at a glance — show next 2 costs, not just next 1
Group Echo upgrades by mechanical theme (faith, prophet, veil, era gates, etc.) with unlabeled visual separation
Tree must not require scrolling to see all currently relevant options

post_final_improvements.title: Act System Result Clarity
scope: Show projected belief_gained before committing to an act, using current B/s and act_duration
Post-act result displayed in omen log with narrative framing, not raw formula output
Domain match bonus should be visibly reflected in the projection (e.g., 'resonant domain' indicator)
act_floor echo upgrade effect should be visible when active
Acts with low projected return should not be hidden, but de-emphasized in ordering

post_final_improvements.title: Offline Return Narrative Polish
scope: Offline return summary should lead with a single evocative in-world sentence before showing deltas
Deltas presented in order of narrative weight: followers, prophets, veil change, belief gained
Avoid showing raw seconds elapsed; use human-readable duration (e.g., '3 hours, 14 minutes')
If Veil stability or follower flow changed significantly offline, surface that prominently as a priority action for return
Summary screen should be dismissible with one tap/click and not reappear until next offline session

post_final_improvements.title: Domain Synergy Feedback
scope: Show active domain_synergy bonus visibly when cult output is being calculated
Matching domain pairs highlighted in domain panel (subtle, not noisy)
Tooltip or expandable section showing which pairs are matched and their synergy contribution
When a new cult is formed or a domain is invested in, briefly surface whether synergy changed
Keeps domain investment decisions feeling meaningful rather than opaque

post_final_improvements.title: Veil Mastery Zone Indicator
scope: Veil meter should visually communicate the three zones: safe (above 55), optimal (30-55), danger (below 30)
Zone coloring or texture changes, not just a number — the player should feel the zone, not calculate it
Current veil_bonus multiplier displayed as a live value next to or within the meter
Collapse threshold marked on the meter with a subtle indicator (becomes visible after first collapse or Era III unlock)
Zone indicators follow progressive reveal: safe/optimal distinction unlocks in Era II, danger zone in Era III

post_final_improvements.title: Pantheon Relationship State Legibility
scope: Each Pantheon entity shows current disposition (ally / neutral / hostile) with one-line contextual flavor
Betrayal history flagged permanently on the entity entry — in-universe language, not 'BETRAYED' label
Domain poisoning effect visible on affected domains with source attribution
Alliance benefits summarized in one line per entity, not buried in stat deltas
Pantheon panel hidden until run 2 per era-locked disclosure rules

post_final_improvements.title: Remembrance Conditions Tracker
scope: All 8 Name Letter conditions tracked in a persistent, cross-run panel
Conditions shown as in-world fragments, not mechanical labels — e.g., 'A domain remembered beyond forgetting' not 'Domain level 10'
Completed conditions visually distinct; uncompleted ones show progress where quantifiable (e.g., 3/5 civ rebuilds)
Panel hidden until at least one condition has been completed or the player has completed run 1
Conditions that require specific runs or Pantheon actions flagged as requiring preparation, without spoiling exact mechanic

post_final_improvements.title: Miracle Naming - Replace Tier Labels
post_final_improvements.notes: Miracle naming scope absorbed into PF-25, which combines it with the Veil pressure rebalance. Do not implement PF-21 separately.
scope: Replace Miracle T1 with an in-world name reflecting subtle, low-cost divine touch (e.g. Whisper of Providence)
Replace Miracle T2 with an in-world name reflecting ritualistic, meaningful intervention (e.g. The Anointing)
Replace Miracle T3 with an in-world name reflecting violent, costly world-shaping (e.g. The Rending)
Replace Miracle T4 with an in-world name reflecting catastrophic, near-unraveling force (e.g. Unraveling - echoes the gate name intentionally)
Names applied everywhere tier is referenced: Cataclysm panel buttons, omen log entries, stats page miracle count line
Cost, return, Veil cost, and Civ damage values unchanged

post_final_improvements.title: Influence Cap Scaling - Era III Late Game
post_final_improvements.notes: Implemented. Era III Influence cap now scales with cult count, average domain level, and shrine count to prevent late-game under-cap bottlenecks.
scope: Add a cult-count term to the Influence cap formula for Era III: +5 per cult above 3
Add an optional domain-level term: +2 per point of average domain level above 5
Add shrine scaling term for Era III: +0.5 per shrine above 20
Reference target: player with 18 prophets, 26 cults, domain avg level 6 should have cap ~700-800
Cap scaling must not trivialize Miracle costs - cap growth should trail regen growth so spend-before-cap behavior remains relevant
Update influenceCap formula in MANIFESTO.md and GAME_REFERENCE.md in same change
Validate that fill time from 0 remains meaningful (target: 10-20 seconds at Era III regen rates)

post_final_improvements.title: Active Act Visual State - Running vs Available
post_final_improvements.notes: Currently active/running acts are not visually distinct from available acts. When an act is in progress it should be immediately identifiable with a countdown and visual differentiation. Slot count header updates correctly but the cards themselves do not communicate state.
scope: Running act cards render with a countdown timer showing time remaining
Running act cards are visually differentiated from available ones: dimmed or shifted background, progress bar underlay, or equivalent
Available act cards remain full opacity and interactive
Completed act resolution fires an omen line as currently specified
Slot count header (e.g. '6 of 26 slots active') correctly reflects running count at all times
No changes to act formulas, costs, or return calculations

post_final_improvements.title: Era III UI Consistency Pass
post_final_improvements.notes: Several small inconsistencies visible in Era III runtime that conflict with the UI restructure spec and string replacement doc. Collect here to be addressed in one pass.
scope: Unraveling Gate label appears twice (section header and inner bar header) - inner bar label should read 'Ready to Unravel' when conditions unmet, 'Cross the Threshold' when all met
Suppress Rival button when no rivals are present should be hidden entirely, not greyed or visible
Veil Stability descriptor (Calm/Thinning/Fraying/Critical/Unraveling) must appear in stat bar in Era III - confirm implementation matches UI_RESTRUCTURE.md spec
Bottom status line must be hidden in Era III per era vocabulary progression spec
Cataclysm panel must appear at top of ACTIVE tab in Era III above Rivals and Whispers - confirm correct order per GAME_REFERENCE.md Era III active order
META tab label must read 'Meta' with no suffix in all eras

post_final_improvements.title: Veil Pressure Rebalance and Miracle Naming
post_final_improvements.notes: Completed baseline pass. Shrine regen now uses diminishing returns, erosion includes shrine pressure, and miracle labels are in-world names. Follow-up numeric miracle retunes are tracked separately under PF-27.
scope: Apply diminishing returns to shrine Veil regen: shrine_regen_per_second = (shrine_count * base_shrine_rate) / (1 + shrine_count * 0.015)
Set base_shrine_rate to 1/90, or 1/80 with veilRegen echo bonus
Add shrine-count erosion term: erosion = 0.001 * log10(totalBeliefEarned) + 0.0002 * shrine_count
Replace Miracle T1 with Whisper of Providence
Replace Miracle T2 with The Anointing
Replace Miracle T3 with The Rending
Replace Miracle T4 with Unraveling
Apply miracle names everywhere tier labels appear: Cataclysm panel, omen log, stats surfaces, and any tier-referenced tooltip/projection text
Update MANIFESTO.md and docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md in the same change with revised Veil formulas and miracle names
Keep non-PF25 miracle numeric retunes tracked in dedicated follow-up items
validation: At high shrine counts, Veil pressure requires active management and does not trivially stay in safe zone
At low shrine counts, early Era III remains meaningfully recoverable between miracles
All four miracle tiers display in-world names everywhere tier labels were shown
Any subsequent miracle numeric retune is documented in a dedicated follow-up item
MANIFESTO.md and docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md match runtime formulas

post_final_improvements.title: Persistent Desktop Shell - Omens Sidebar and Stats Dock
post_final_improvements.notes: Implemented with UX follow-up. On wider viewports the shell uses a persistent OMENS sidebar plus a separate fixed STATS dock. Mobile keeps the existing bottom omen strip and floating stats button. Omen rendering is run-scoped to prevent prior-run bleed and capped for readability.
scope: On viewports >= 800px, render a persistent right OMENS sidebar in all eras
Sidebar width contract: 240px default, 300px on large screens; left column keeps a 500px minimum width
Move OMENS/MURMURS/WHISPERS log surface into the right sidebar on wide viewports
Use a separate fixed top-right STATS dock on wide viewports instead of embedding STATS in the sidebar
Desktop STATS dock defaults collapsed with trigger label `STATS` and no glow treatment
Hide the mobile floating STATS button on wide viewports; keep it on mobile
Keep Stat Bar and Era III Unraveling Gate strip full width above the split
Constrain left content column (Era I single-column and Era II/III tab content) to accommodate sidebar
Give right panel subtle background differentiation while preserving era token styling
Filter active omen surface to current run (`entry.at >= runStartTimestamp`) so prior-run lines do not render in the live surface
Cap stored/rendered omen entries to the latest six (`OMEN_LOG_MAX_ENTRIES = 6`)
Remove desktop-only expand control for the right-panel omen list; the surface remains a short rolling feed
Preserve existing mobile behavior unchanged for viewports < 800px
No changes to formulas; content systems remain unchanged apart from log visibility hygiene and presentation layout
validation: On >= 800px, right OMENS sidebar is visible in Era I, Era II, and Era III
Era header label in sidebar follows vocabulary progression: MURMURS (I), WHISPERS (II), OMENS (III)
On >= 800px, STATS is accessed through the fixed top-right dock and is collapsed by default
On >= 800px, mobile floating STATS button is hidden; on < 800px it is visible
On < 800px, existing omen strip and floating stats behavior remain unchanged
Tab/era content in the left column remains readable with no horizontal overflow
Stat Bar and Era III Unraveling strip remain full width
Active omen surface only shows entries from the current run and never displays prior-run lines
Active omen surface shows at most six recent entries

post_final_improvements.title: Miracle Power Reserve and Legacy Echo Access
post_final_improvements.notes: Implemented as a follow-up balance/access pass after PF-22 and PF-25. Addresses late-era miracle access friction and post-ascension echo investment dead-time.
scope: Introduce Era III miracle reserve pool fed by Influence overflow at cap
Miracle cast affordability checks and costs use total miracle power (Influence + reserve), spending Influence first then reserve
Expose miracle reserve and total miracle power in Cataclysm and Stats surfaces
Retune Whisper of Providence output profile (base gain, Veil cost, civ damage) to reduce overperformance
Add post-ascension Era I Legacy Echoes quick-spend panel so Echo points are investable before returning to Era III
Update MANIFESTO.md, docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md, and regression coverage for the new formulas/behavior
validation: Miracles remain castable through reserve when Influence alone is below cost
Influence overflow at cap accumulates reserve up to reserve cap; reserve does not grow offline
Cataclysm and Stats surfaces show reserve and total miracle power accurately
Whisper of Providence retune reduces early-miracle dominance without changing tier ordering
Echo spending is reachable in Era I after at least one completed run

post_final_improvements.title: Whisper Evolution and Echo Sink Expansion
post_final_improvements.notes: Implemented. Whisper now evolves by era with target/magnitude profiles, cooldown/failure pressure, and ascension-linked reliability scaling. Echo trees now support deeper progression and higher-rank sinks so post-ascension Echo spend cannot be exhausted immediately.
scope: Era II whisper targeting: Crowd (base), Prophets (+8 Influence, +40% follower yield), Cults (+12 Influence, +60% follower yield, cooldown)
Era III whisper magnitudes: retain base options and add boosted variants (Open Proclamation, Sacred Charge, Doctrine Wave) with higher cost, higher yield, higher risk
Whisper strain/failure model: per-target failure chance with reduced follower gain on strained outcomes
Ascension synergy: completed runs reduce whisper failure pressure via multiplicative reliability factor
Doctrine-target cooldown system: cult-target whispers enforce recovery windows with era/magnitude-aware cooldown values
Echo trees expanded from 5 to 12 ranks with formula-based cost scaling for steeper late-rank costs
High-rank echo overflow bonuses: whisper yield bonus, fail-chance reduction, surcharge reduction, cult-target cooldown reduction, boosted-fail mitigation, and miracle reserve cap growth
Update runtime previews/tooltips to show per-option cost, follower outcomes, cooldown, and strain chance
Update MANIFESTO.md and docs/GAME_REFERENCE.md with whisper evolution and echo scaling formulas in the same change
validation: Era I keeps single whisper action behavior with no target split
Era II exposes three whisper targets and enforces cult-target cooldown behavior
Era III exposes both base and boosted whisper variants with distinct cost/yield/risk profiles
Whisper affordability and action gating correctly respect target profile, cooldown, and influence availability
Echo tree purchases continue beyond rank 5 and cannot be trivially maxed in one ascension
High-rank echo investment produces measurable whisper reliability/yield improvements
Documentation formulas match runtime behavior for whisper profiles and echo progression
